programme.title: The Agenda
programme.desc: A weekly talk-show looking beyond the headlines and “accepted wisdom” to find the real story. Hosted by award-winning journalist and presenter Stephen Cole, The Agenda quite simply sets The Agenda . You won’t find the same-old talking heads recycling platitudes; instead thought-provoking guests to debate and explore key issues of today’s world. Using virtual reality graphics to bring subjects to life, Stephen will navigate through subjects ranging from “Prove That Climate Change Exists” to “Who Will Win the Race to Space”. Opposing views will be given opportunity to flourish and develop into a meaningful conversation. The Agenda: No nonsense talk; Challenging perceptions.

programme.title: Razor
programme.desc: CGTN's brand new science and technology show, covering cutting edge developments from all over the world. RAZOR will show you the world's most powerful laser and the world's smallest anti-cancer treatment. It will take you from the weird world of quantum computing to the boundaries of the cosmos. An international team of reporters will follow the guidance of Occam's Razor - the principle that the simplest explanation is the best one. RAZOR will show that we are already ""living in the future"!

programme.title: Best Eats
programme.desc: Food connects people! A feast for the eyes that spotlights the cuisines of China in a way that’s bound to set the mouths of the whole globe watering!

programme.title: Youthful China
programme.desc: The youth of a country is the future of that country. A series of documentaries focusing on the younger generation of China in which you get to see what they are achieving and what makes them tick!

programme.title: Pixel Collage
programme.desc: A well-crafted collage of selected short documentaries telling stories about all aspects of China! People, technology, culture, and wildlife, all are showcased in Internet-friendly form.

programme.title: Today’s Top Pick
programme.desc: Documentaries in this slot are rebroadcasts of our most-viewed and most popular stories of the season. They feature important social, economic, environmental or other policy-driven issues, affecting lives nationwide or simply in one small village. A broad selection of top productions by Chinese top documentary producers.

programme.title: Humanities and Geography
programme.desc: A strand devoted to the nature and wildlife of China, the people who lived alongside them, and the great and diverse landscapes with which they all contend.

programme.title: Living China
programme.desc: Real life stories told from the personal perspective. Moving, dramatic, sorrows and joys, expressed emphatically and expressed quietly. Watch closely to see and understand the real China as it is lived.

programme.title: Special Edition
programme.desc: Prestige Chinese documentary production, tackling serious subjects with the best cinematography, editing, and all round production values with a unique Chinese perspective.

programme.title: Journeys and Discoveries
programme.desc: A documentary series showcasing the history of China and its people.

programme.title: History in the Frame
programme.desc: A documentary series featuring historical and archaeological exploration.

programme.title: DOCUMENTAL
programme.desc: Presentapersonajes y acontecimientos típicos, avances y descubrimientos tecnológicos y científicos, y la civilización social de China.

programme.title: ASÍ ES CHINA
programme.desc: Descubre para el televidente el gran legado cultural e histórico de las distintas regiones y etnias de China. También le acerca a la vida y las costumbres de la gente común y le muestra la evolución del país en aspectos como educación, sociedad y protección del medio ambiente y del patrimonio histórico y natural.

programme.title: CGTN NOTICIAS

programme.title: HORA INFANTIL
programme.desc: Una selección de las mejores series chinas de dibujos animados, dobladas al español. Hora Infantil es una ventana a través de la cual los niños y adolescentes de los países de habla hispana pueden llegar a comprender mejor qué es y cómo es China.

programme.title: MIRADA ECONÓMICA
programme.desc: Invita a expertos chinos en asuntos de los países hispanohablantes y a analistas de esos países para que den a conocer sus opiniones y visiones acerca de los temas de contingencia.

programme.title: EL PROYECTOR
programme.desc: Acérquese a la vida de la China antigua y moderna a través de las producciones dramáticas de CGTN-Español. La combinación perfecta de historia y ficción chinas en la pantalla de su televisor.

programme.title: CGTN NOTICIAS
programme.desc: Es el informativo estelar del canal, con las últimas noticias del acontecer político, social, deportivo y cultural de China y el resto del mundo. Los hechos que constituyen titulares están aquí, en CGTN Noticias.

programme.title: SABOREANDO CHINA
programme.desc: Aprenda a preparar las recetas más populares de China. Nuestro chef le revelará de forma práctica los secretos del arte culinario de esta nación asiática. Conozca los ingredientes más utilizados en la variada cocina regional, los orígenes de los platos e incluso sus beneficios para la salud. En De China a tu cocina, el espectador podrá experimentar a la vez la riqueza tradicional y la variedad gastronómica de China.

programme.title: NIHAO CHINA
programme.desc: Es la clase de chino de CGTN-Español. Las series “Aprendamos juntos pinyin”, “Comunícate en chino” y “Viajando y aprendiendo chino”, abordan diferentes niveles de aprendizaje de la nueva lengua de moda en el mundo, brindando así a los televidentes la posibilidad de ampliar su vocabulario, mejorar su gramática y dominar las frases y expresiones más usuales del idioma con más hablantes en el planeta.

programme.title: DOCUMENTAL
programme.desc: Presentapersonajes y acontecimientos típicos, avances y descubrimientos tecnológicos y científicos, y la civilización social de China.

programme.desc: Descubre para el televidente el gran legado cultural e histórico de las distintas regiones y etnias de China. programme.title: PRISMA
programme.desc: Prisma es un programa cultural producido por CGTN en español en colaboración con el multimedio de comunicación latinoamericano teleSUR. Su objetivo es mostrar a los televidentes la riqueza cultural de China y el mundo hispanohablante, elevar el conocimiento de la cultura china en los países de habla hispana y establecer una plataforma de diálogo entre ambas partes. Se trata de un programa mensual de 30 minutos de duración que comenzó a emitirse en agosto de 2016. Está compuesto de tres segmentos que destacan por su diversidad temática. Primero, un espacio de noticias culturales; después, una semblanza de alguna personalidad del ámbito cultural latinoamericano; y finalmente un documental sobre aspectos de la cultura china.

programme.title: RONDA ARTÍSTICA
programme.desc: Se exhibe la diversidad de cantos y danzas, óperas locales tradicionales, música y artes folklóricas de China.

Las series “Aprendamos juntos pinyin”, “Comunícate en chino” y “Viajando y aprendiendo chino”, abordan diferentes niveles de aprendizaje de la nueva lengua de moda en el mundo, brindando así a los televidentes la posibilidad de ampliar su vocabulario, mejorar su gramática y dominar las frases y expresiones más usuales del idioma con más hablantes en el planeta.

programme.title: DOCUMENTAL
programme.desc: Presentapersonajes y acontecimientos típicos, avances y descubrimientos tecnológicos y científicos, y la civilización social de China.

programme.title: ASÍ ES CHINA
programme.desc: Descubre para el televidente el gran legado cultural e histórico de las distintas regiones y etnias de China. También le acerca a la vida y las costumbres de la gente común y le muestra la evolución del país en aspectos como educación, sociedad y protección del medio ambiente y del patrimonio histórico y natural.

programme.title: CGTN NOTICIAS
programme.desc: Es el informativo estelar del canal, con las últimas noticias del acontecer político, social, deportivo y cultural de China y el resto del mundo. Los hechos que constituyen titulares están aquí, en CGTN Noticias.

programme.title: PRISMA
programme.desc: Prisma es un programa cultural producido por CGTN en español en colaboración con el multimedio de comunicación latinoamericano teleSUR. Su objetivo es mostrar a los televidentes la riqueza cultural de China y el mundo hispanohablante, elevar el conocimiento de la cultura china en los países de habla hispana y establecer una plataforma de diálogo entre ambas partes. Se trata de un programa mensual de 30 minutos de duración que comenzó a emitirse en agosto de 2016. Está compuesto de tres segmentos que destacan por su diversidad temática. Primero, un espacio de noticias culturales; después, una semblanza de alguna personalidad del ámbito cultural latinoamericano; y finalmente un documental sobre aspectos de la cultura china.

programme.title: CGTN NOTICIAS
programme.desc: Es el informativo estelar del canal, con las últimas noticias del acontecer político, social, deportivo y cultural de China y el resto del mundo. Los hechos que constituyen titulares están aquí, en CGTN Noticias.

programme.title: HORA INFANTIL
programme.desc: Una selección de las mejores series chinas de dibujos animados, dobladas al español. Hora Infantil es una ventana a través de la cual los niños y adolescentes de los países de habla hispana pueden llegar a comprender mejor qué es y cómo es China.

programme.title: CAFÉ DE CGTN
programme.desc: En este espacio, los compañeros de la familia de CGTN en español, intercambian opiniones y debaten de forma casual los temas económicos, sociales y culturales de mayor actualidad en China.

programme.title: EL PROYECTOR
programme.desc: Acérquese a la vida de la China antigua y moderna a través de las producciones dramáticas de CGTN-Español. La combinación perfecta de historia y ficción chinas en la pantalla de su televisor.

programme.title: CGTN NOTICIAS
programme.desc: Es el informativo estelar del canal, con las últimas noticias del acontecer político, social, deportivo y cultural de China y el resto del mundo. Los hechos que constituyen titulares están aquí, en CGTN Noticias.

programme.title: SABOREANDO CHINA
programme.desc: Aprenda a preparar las recetas más populares de China. Nuestro chef le revelará de forma práctica los secretos del arte culinario de esta nación asiática. Conozca los ingredientes más utilizados en la variada cocina regional, los orígenes de los platos e incluso sus beneficios para la salud. En De China a tu cocina, el espectador podrá experimentar a la vez la riqueza tradicional y la variedad gastronómica de China.

programme.title: AMÉRICA AHORA
programme.desc: Es un programa nuevo del canal, de corte noticioso con estilo de revista, y que se transmite una vez a la semana. Aquí ofrecemos reportajes y análisis en profundidad sobre los temas más debatidos y de mayor actualidad en la zona americana, y en especial en América Latina.

programme.title: DÍALOGO
programme.desc: Convoca a celebridades, eruditos y funcionarios gubernamentales tanto de China como del exterior, para debatir temas de palpitante actualidad e interés general y de proyección internacional.

Conozca los ingredientes más utilizados en la variada cocina regional, los orígenes de los platos e incluso sus beneficios para la salud. En De China a tu cocina, el espectador podrá experimentar a la vez la riqueza tradicional y la variedad gastronómica de China.

programme.title: NIHAO CHINA
programme.desc: Es la clase de chino de CGTN-Español. Las series “Aprendamos juntos pinyin”, “Comunícate en chino” y “Viajando y aprendiendo chino”, abordan diferentes niveles de aprendizaje de la nueva lengua de moda en el mundo, brindando así a los televidentes la posibilidad de ampliar su vocabulario, mejorar su gramática y dominar las frases y expresiones más usuales del idioma con más hablantes en el planeta.

programme.title: RONDA ARTÍSTICA
programme.desc: Se exhibe la diversidad de cantos y danzas, óperas locales tradicionales, música y artes folklóricas de China.

programme.title: DIARIOS DE VIAJE
programme.desc: Es un espacio que muestra al televidente la gran diversidad existente en China a través de entretenidos y cautivadores viajes alrededor del país. En Diarios de Viaje nuestros televidentes encontrarán hermosos paisajes y destinos de incalculable valor histórico, turístico y cultural. Modernidad y tradición, el presente y el pasado se unen en un solo programa.

programme.title: DÍALOGO
programme.desc: Convoca a celebridades, eruditos y funcionarios gubernamentales tanto de China como del exterior, para debatir temas de palpitante actualidad e interés general y de proyección internacional.

programme.title: DOCUMENTAL
programme.desc: Presentapersonajes y acontecimientos típicos, avances y descubrimientos tecnológicos y científicos, y la civilización social de China.

programme.title: ASÍ ES CHINA
programme.desc: Descubre para el televidente el gran legado cultural e histórico de las distintas regiones y etnias de China. También le acerca a la vida y las costumbres de la gente común y le muestra la evolución del país en aspectos como educación, sociedad y protección del medio ambiente y del patrimonio histórico y natural.

En De China a tu cocina, el espectador podrá experimentar a la vez la riqueza tradicional y la variedad gastronómica de China.

programme.title: NIHAO CHINA
programme.desc: Es la clase de chino de CGTN-Español. Las series “Aprendamos juntos pinyin”, “Comunícate en chino” y “Viajando y aprendiendo chino”, abordan diferentes niveles de aprendizaje de la nueva lengua de moda en el mundo, brindando así a los televidentes la posibilidad de ampliar su vocabulario, mejorar su gramática y dominar las frases y expresiones más usuales del idioma con más hablantes en el planeta.

programme.title: DOCUMENTAL
programme.desc: Presentapersonajes y acontecimientos típicos, avances y descubrimientos tecnológicos y científicos, y la civilización social de China.

programme.title: ASÍ ES CHINA
programme.desc: Descubre para el televidente el gran legado cultural e histórico de las distintas regiones y etnias de China. También le acerca a la vida y las costumbres de la gente común y le muestra la evolución del país en aspectos como educación, sociedad y protección del medio ambiente y del patrimonio histórico y natural.

programme.title: CGTN NOTICIAS
programme.desc: Es el informativo estelar del canal, con las últimas noticias del acontecer político, social, deportivo y cultural de China y el resto del mundo. Los hechos que constituyen titulares están aquí, en CGTN Noticias.

programme.title: HORA INFANTIL
programme.desc: Una selección de las mejores series chinas de dibujos animados, dobladas al español. Hora Infantil es una ventana a través de la cual los niños y adolescentes de los países de habla hispana pueden llegar a comprender mejor qué es y cómo es China.

programme.title: DÍALOGO
programme.desc: Convoca a celebridades, eruditos y funcionarios gubernamentales tanto de China como del exterior, para debatir temas de palpitante actualidad e interés general y de proyección internacional.

What’s the future like for the “token economy”? Join us on BizTalk as we explore the economics behind the AI revolution.

programme.title: BizTalk | China's mid-year economic review
programme.desc:
How did China's economy perform in the first half of the year? What opportunities and challenges lie ahead in achieving this year's growth target?
In this episode of BizTalk, CGTN's Lily Lyu sits down with experts from Morgan Stanley, China International Capital Corporation and the Central University of Finance and Economics to break down China’s latest economic data, explore the outlook for the economy, emerging trends and policy priorities in the second half of the year.

programme.title: BizTalk | China's mid-year economic review
programme.desc:
How did China's economy perform in the first half of the year? What opportunities and challenges lie ahead in achieving this year's growth target?
In this episode of BizTalk, CGTN's Lily Lyu sits down with experts from Morgan Stanley, China International Capital Corporation and the Central University of Finance and Economics to break down China’s latest economic data, explore the outlook for the economy, emerging trends and policy priorities in the second half of the year.

programme.title: BizPodcast: Decoding China's H1 2026 economic transformation
programme.desc: Is China's economy stalling, or are we witnessing the most significant structural pivot in modern economic history? In this episode, we look past the headline GDP numbers to unpack China's H1 2026 economic data. (AI-generated audio)

programme.title: RAZOR: Soil Regeneration
programme.desc: The soil on our planet is not just vital to grow the food we eat, but also plays an important role in the intricate balance of above and below-ground ecological systems. This has been degraded worldwide at a staggering rate, largely due to unsustainable agriculture, ongoing deforestation, and climate change. In Switzerland, the Crowther Lab is taking a big picture approach to understanding the holistic structure of global ecosystems. RAZOR’s Amelia Martyn Hemphill heads out to the forest near Zurich with lab lead Tom Crowther to find out about what they are working on to help restore degraded soil around the world. One of the major factors of soil degradation is industrialized farming. However, around the world there is a growing appreciation for the powers of regenerative agricultural practices. RAZOR travels to Cornwall to meet chef Dan Cox and farmer Tim Williams at Crocadon Farm. ‘Regen’ farmers work to actively change the way they farm by increasing biodiversity, enriching soils and enhancing the health of livestock and wildlife.

programme.title: RAZOR: DNA Archaeology
programme.desc: In this edition of RAZOR, Neil Cairns investigates a breakthrough known as "DNA archaeology" — a non-destructive way of retrieving ancient human DNA from Paleolithic tools. At La Grande Roche cave in France, archaeologist Dr. Marie Soressi shows how stone artifacts can now be directly linked to the people who once used them. In Leipzig, geneticist Elena Essel demonstrates the delicate extraction process inside a contamination-free clean lab, soaking artifacts in a phosphate buffer to release microscopic DNA fragments without damaging the object. Finally, Dr. Matthias Meyer analyses the sequences against modern reference genomes, uncovering details of sex, ancestry, and social patterns in Ice Age communities. For the first time, scientists are connecting tools not just to cultures, but to individuals — transforming our understanding of ancient human life.

programme.title: No Power, No AI
programme.desc: Is energy the ultimate bottleneck for artificial intelligence? As compute demand skyrockets, can our power grids keep up? CGTN's Michael Wang moderates a critical panel at the 2026 Summer Davos Forum on the intersection of AI and energy infrastructure. We'll explore how to align capital investment, infrastructure build-out, and policy frameworks; future-proof energy supply systems to strengthen energy security; and build a more inclusive, sustainable AI-powered energy future. Tune in as global leaders tackle the energy challenge powering the next wave of AI. #SummerDavos2026

programme.title: What's under the hood of a quantum computer?
programme.desc: Leading scientists have been seeking a new holy grail – "quantum supremacy." The term "quantum supremacy" does not indicate any technological superiority of one country over another, but rather the enormous computing advantage of quantum computers over classical computers, the latter can vary from the office laptop you type on to those supercomputers that require an entire building to house.

programme.title: New life for world cultural heritage
programme.desc: A sad day for humanity as a massive fire spread throughout Paris' Notre-Dame Cathedral on Monday. The blaze gulf the building's spire and roof to collapse. The Paris landmark has seen numerous restorations in its 800-plus-year history, but this time, a rebuild is needed, as pledged by France President Emmanuel Macron. But how? The only news not that bad today is that for years, architectural historians worked with laser scanners to capture the entirety of the cathedral's interior and exterior in meticulous 3D point clouds, a technology called 3D mapping. In season 2 of CGTN's original series Tech It Out, we reported this technology used by scientists to protect fragile world heritage in China. We hope this will make your day a little bit easier.

programme.title: FPV, the future racing
programme.desc: First-person-view (FPV) drone racing, the art of flying drones around obstacle courses at incredible speeds (180 to 200 Kilometers per hour) and using head-mounted displays and joysticks to control their direction, has started to emerge as a new sport in China. Since the first race began in China, FPV has attracted a loyal fan base, among them are a number of professional pilots who intend to turn this novelty concept into a mainstream sport.

programme.title: Tracing indoor location signals
programme.desc: We use positioning services in our everyday lives. For instance, the GPS module built into smartphones can receive signals from satellites that are 25,000 kilometers or higher above the earth. But the accuracy of indoor positioning is still unreliable because it is hard for satellite signals to pass through concrete walls. Chen Ruizhi, professor at the State Key Laboratory of Information Engineering in Surveying, Mapping and Remote Sensing in Wuhan, China’s Hubei Province, demonstrated the real-time indoor positioning technology to Tech It Out through a smartphone. The service can be used inside of the lab with a margin of error of less than two meters, which is much more accurate than an outdoor GPS.

programme.title: Meet China: Turning Villages into Stages, Taking Creativity to the World
programme.desc: In this episode of Meet China, we step into the frontlines of rural revitalization to see how a Gen-Z village chief is breathing new life into her hometown. We then visit the world’s toy capital, where Chinese manufacturing is turning culture and creativity into global stories. From a green beef cycle near Beijing to rice-prawn co-culture in Cambodia, this episode explores how technology, industry and cooperation are helping land thrive and livelihoods grow.

programme.title: Beyond the screen: how “film ” tourism and AI redefine moviegoer experience
programme.desc: As the Beijing International Film Festival takes over the city, films are no longer just something we watch in cinemas. They’re connecting tourism, retail, entertainment, and even cutting-edge tech. What does this “film plus” experience look like for audiences today? And as AI reshapes the film industry, what opportunities and challenges lie ahead? Host Xu Yawen spoke with Li Lun, assistant professor of economics at Peking University; Geng Ling, film producer and CEO of China Film Assist Company; and Teng Jimeng, associate professor of American studies at Beijing Foreign Studies University, to delve into these questions.

programme.title: Beyond the screen: how “film ” tourism and AI redefine moviegoer experience
programme.desc: As the Beijing International Film Festival takes over the city, films are no longer just something we watch in cinemas. They’re connecting tourism, retail, entertainment, and even cutting-edge tech. What does this “film plus” experience look like for audiences today? programme.title: What can Africa learn from China’s poverty reduction experience?
programme.desc: Five years after China eliminated absolute poverty, its development experience continues to attract attention around the world, particularly across Africa. What lessons can African countries draw from China’s journey? Can China’s poverty reduction experiences be adapted to African realities? In today’s episode, we bring scholars, journalists and development practitioners from China, The Gambia, Rwanda and Namibia. Jainaba Sonko, Zhang Yanqiu, Patrick Rurangangabo and Jeronimo Kateya explore pathways to sustainable development and poverty reduction, discussing governance, infrastructure, education, self-reliance and the future of China-Africa cooperation.

Finally, Dr. Matthias Meyer analyses the sequences against modern reference genomes, uncovering details of sex, ancestry, and social patterns in Ice Age communities. For the first time, scientists are connecting tools not just to cultures, but to individuals — transforming our understanding of ancient human life.

programme.title: No Power, No AI
programme.desc: Is energy the ultimate bottleneck for artificial intelligence? As compute demand skyrockets, can our power grids keep up? CGTN's Michael Wang moderates a critical panel at the 2026 Summer Davos Forum on the intersection of AI and energy infrastructure. We'll explore how to align capital investment, infrastructure build-out, and policy frameworks; future-proof energy supply systems to strengthen energy security; and build a more inclusive, sustainable AI-powered energy future. Tune in as global leaders tackle the energy challenge powering the next wave of AI. #SummerDavos2026

programme.title: What's under the hood of a quantum computer?
programme.desc: Leading scientists have been seeking a new holy grail – "quantum supremacy." The term "quantum supremacy" does not indicate any technological superiority of one country over another, but rather the enormous computing advantage of quantum computers over classical computers, the latter can vary from the office laptop you type on to those supercomputers that require an entire building to house.

programme.title: New life for world cultural heritage
programme.desc: A sad day for humanity as a massive fire spread throughout Paris' Notre-Dame Cathedral on Monday. The blaze gulf the building's spire and roof to collapse. The Paris landmark has seen numerous restorations in its 800-plus-year history, but this time, a rebuild is needed, as pledged by France President Emmanuel Macron. But how? The only news not that bad today is that for years, architectural historians worked with laser scanners to capture the entirety of the cathedral's interior and exterior in meticulous 3D point clouds, a technology called 3D mapping. In season 2 of CGTN's original series Tech It Out, we reported this technology used by scientists to protect fragile world heritage in China. We hope this will make your day a little bit easier.

programme.title: FPV, the future racing
programme.desc: First-person-view (FPV) drone racing, the art of flying drones around obstacle courses at incredible speeds (180 to 200 Kilometers per hour) and using head-mounted displays and joysticks to control their direction, has started to emerge as a new sport in China. Since the first race began in China, FPV has attracted a loyal fan base, among them are a number of professional pilots who intend to turn this novelty concept into a mainstream sport.

programme.title: Tracing indoor location signals
programme.desc: We use positioning services in our everyday lives. For instance, the GPS module built into smartphones can receive signals from satellites that are 25,000 kilometers or higher above the earth. But the accuracy of indoor positioning is still unreliable because it is hard for satellite signals to pass through concrete walls. Chen Ruizhi, professor at the State Key Laboratory of Information Engineering in Surveying, Mapping and Remote Sensing in Wuhan, China’s Hubei Province, demonstrated the real-time indoor positioning technology to Tech It Out through a smartphone. The service can be used inside of the lab with a margin of error of less than two meters, which is much more accurate than an outdoor GPS.

programme.title: Meet China: Turning Villages into Stages, Taking Creativity to the World
programme.desc: In this episode of Meet China, we step into the frontlines of rural revitalization to see how a Gen-Z village chief is breathing new life into her hometown. We then visit the world’s toy capital, where Chinese manufacturing is turning culture and creativity into global stories. From a green beef cycle near Beijing to rice-prawn co-culture in Cambodia, this episode explores how technology, industry and cooperation are helping land thrive and livelihoods grow.

programme.title: Beyond the screen: how “film ” tourism and AI redefine moviegoer experience
programme.desc: As the Beijing International Film Festival takes over the city, films are no longer just something we watch in cinemas. They’re connecting tourism, retail, entertainment, and even cutting-edge tech. What does this “film plus” experience look like for audiences today? And as AI reshapes the film industry, what opportunities and challenges lie ahead? Host Xu Yawen spoke with Li Lun, assistant professor of economics at Peking University; Geng Ling, film producer and CEO of China Film Assist Company; and Teng Jimeng, associate professor of American studies at Beijing Foreign Studies University, to delve into these questions.

programme.title: Beyond the screen: how “film ” tourism and AI redefine moviegoer experience

programme.title: Beyond the screen: how “film ” tourism and AI redefine moviegoer experience

And as AI reshapes the film industry, what opportunities and challenges lie ahead? Host Xu Yawen spoke with Li Lun, assistant professor of economics at Peking University; Geng Ling, film producer and CEO of China Film Assist Company; and Teng Jimeng, associate professor of American studies at Beijing Foreign Studies University, to delve into these questions.

programme.title: What can Africa learn from China’s poverty reduction experience?
programme.desc: Five years after China eliminated absolute poverty, its development experience continues to attract attention around the world, particularly across Africa. What lessons can African countries draw from China’s journey? Can China’s poverty reduction experiences be adapted to African realities? In today’s episode, we bring scholars, journalists and development practitioners from China, The Gambia, Rwanda and Namibia. Jainaba Sonko, Zhang Yanqiu, Patrick Rurangangabo and Jeronimo Kateya explore pathways to sustainable development and poverty reduction, discussing governance, infrastructure, education, self-reliance and the future of China-Africa cooperation.

programme.title: What can Africa learn from China’s poverty reduction experience?
programme.desc: Five years after China eliminated absolute poverty, its development experience continues to attract attention around the world, particularly across Africa. Jainaba Sonko, Zhang Yanqiu, Patrick Rurangangabo and Jeronimo Kateya explore pathways to sustainable development and poverty reduction, discussing governance, infrastructure, education, self-reliance and the future of China-Africa cooperation.

programme.title: Football expert: AI will never replace referees or players
programme.desc: Artificial intelligence (AI) is playing an increasingly important role at the 2026 World Cup. However, Chris Van Puyvelde, Chinese Football Association Technical Director, said that AI as a tool will never replace players or referees, as the human brain is the ultimate decision-maker in football, in a #Bizpodcast show with CGTN. #BizFocus

programme.title: BizTalk | China's mid-year economic review
programme.desc:
How did China's economy perform in the first half of the year? What opportunities and challenges lie ahead in achieving this year's growth target?
In this episode of BizTalk, CGTN's Lily Lyu sits down with experts from Morgan Stanley, China International Capital Corporation and the Central University of Finance and Economics to break down China’s latest economic data, explore the outlook for the economy, emerging trends and policy priorities in the second half of the year.

programme.title: BizTalk | China's mid-year economic review

programme.title: BizPodcast: Decoding China's H1 2026 economic transformation
programme.desc: Is China's economy stalling, or are we witnessing the most significant structural pivot in modern economic history? In this episode, we look past the headline GDP numbers to unpack China's H1 2026 economic data. (AI-generated audio)

programme.title: RAZOR: Soil Regeneration
programme.desc: The soil on our planet is not just vital to grow the food we eat, but also plays an important role in the intricate balance of above and below-ground ecological systems. This has been degraded worldwide at a staggering rate, largely due to unsustainable agriculture, ongoing deforestation, and climate change. In Switzerland, the Crowther Lab is taking a big picture approach to understanding the holistic structure of global ecosystems. RAZOR’s Amelia Martyn Hemphill heads out to the forest near Zurich with lab lead Tom Crowther to find out about what they are working on to help restore degraded soil around the world. One of the major factors of soil degradation is industrialized farming. However, around the world there is a growing appreciation for the powers of regenerative agricultural practices. RAZOR travels to Cornwall to meet chef Dan Cox and farmer Tim Williams at Crocadon Farm. ‘Regen’ farmers work to actively change the way they farm by increasing biodiversity, enriching soils and enhancing the health of livestock and wildlife.

programme.title: RAZOR: DNA Archaeology
programme.desc: In this edition of RAZOR, Neil Cairns investigates a breakthrough known as "DNA archaeology" — a non-destructive way of retrieving ancient human DNA from Paleolithic tools. At La Grande Roche cave in France, archaeologist Dr. Marie Soressi shows how stone artifacts can now be directly linked to the people who once used them. In Leipzig, geneticist Elena Essel demonstrates the delicate extraction process inside a contamination-free clean lab, soaking artifacts in a phosphate buffer to release microscopic DNA fragments without damaging the object. Finally, Dr. Matthias Meyer analyses the sequences against modern reference genomes, uncovering details of sex, ancestry, and social patterns in Ice Age communities. For the first time, scientists are connecting tools not just to cultures, but to individuals — transforming our understanding of ancient human life.

programme.desc: First-person-view (FPV) drone racing, the art of flying drones around obstacle courses at incredible speeds (180 to 200 Kilometers per hour) and using head-mounted displays and joysticks to control their direction, has started to emerge as a new sport in China. Since the first race began in China, FPV has attracted a loyal fan base, among them are a number of professional pilots who intend to turn this novelty concept into a mainstream sport.

programme.title: Tracing indoor location signals
programme.desc: We use positioning services in our everyday lives. For instance, the GPS module built into smartphones can receive signals from satellites that are 25,000 kilometers or higher above the earth. But the accuracy of indoor positioning is still unreliable because it is hard for satellite signals to pass through concrete walls. Chen Ruizhi, professor at the State Key Laboratory of Information Engineering in Surveying, Mapping and Remote Sensing in Wuhan, China’s Hubei Province, demonstrated the real-time indoor positioning technology to Tech It Out through a smartphone. The service can be used inside of the lab with a margin of error of less than two meters, which is much more accurate than an outdoor GPS.

programme.title: Meet China: Turning Villages into Stages, Taking Creativity to the World
programme.desc: In this episode of Meet China, we step into the frontlines of rural revitalization to see how a Gen-Z village chief is breathing new life into her hometown. We then visit the world’s toy capital, where Chinese manufacturing is turning culture and creativity into global stories. From a green beef cycle near Beijing to rice-prawn co-culture in Cambodia, this episode explores how technology, industry and cooperation are helping land thrive and livelihoods grow.

programme.title: Beyond the screen: how “film ” tourism and AI redefine moviegoer experience
programme.desc: As the Beijing International Film Festival takes over the city, films are no longer just something we watch in cinemas. They’re connecting tourism, retail, entertainment, and even cutting-edge tech. What does this “film plus” experience look like for audiences today? And as AI reshapes the film industry, what opportunities and challenges lie ahead? Host Xu Yawen spoke with Li Lun, assistant professor of economics at Peking University; Geng Ling, film producer and CEO of China Film Assist Company; and Teng Jimeng, associate professor of American studies at Beijing Foreign Studies University, to delve into these questions.

programme.title: What can Africa learn from China’s poverty reduction experience?
programme.desc: Five years after China eliminated absolute poverty, its development experience continues to attract attention around the world, particularly across Africa. What lessons can African countries draw from China’s journey? Can China’s poverty reduction experiences be adapted to African realities? In today’s episode, we bring scholars, journalists and development practitioners from China, The Gambia, Rwanda and Namibia. Jainaba Sonko, Zhang Yanqiu, Patrick Rurangangabo and Jeronimo Kateya explore pathways to sustainable development and poverty reduction, discussing governance, infrastructure, education, self-reliance and the future of China-Africa cooperation.

programme.title: BizTalk | China's mid-year economic review
programme.desc:
How did China's economy perform in the first half of the year? What opportunities and challenges lie ahead in achieving this year's growth target?
In this episode of BizTalk, CGTN's Lily Lyu sits down with experts from Morgan Stanley, China International Capital Corporation and the Central University of Finance and Economics to break down China’s latest economic data, explore the outlook for the economy, emerging trends and policy priorities in the second half of the year.

programme.title: BizTalk | China's mid-year economic review
programme.desc:

programme.title: BizPodcast: Decoding China's H1 2026 economic transformation
programme.desc: Is China's economy stalling, or are we witnessing the most significant structural pivot in modern economic history? In this episode, we look past the headline GDP numbers to unpack China's H1 2026 economic data. (AI-generated audio)

programme.title: RAZOR: Soil Regeneration
programme.desc: The soil on our planet is not just vital to grow the food we eat, but also plays an important role in the intricate balance of above and below-ground ecological systems. This has been degraded worldwide at a staggering rate, largely due to unsustainable agriculture, ongoing deforestation, and climate change. In Switzerland, the Crowther Lab is taking a big picture approach to understanding the holistic structure of global ecosystems. RAZOR’s Amelia Martyn Hemphill heads out to the forest near Zurich with lab lead Tom Crowther to find out about what they are working on to help restore degraded soil around the world. One of the major factors of soil degradation is industrialized farming. However, around the world there is a growing appreciation for the powers of regenerative agricultural practices. RAZOR travels to Cornwall to meet chef Dan Cox and farmer Tim Williams at Crocadon Farm. ‘Regen’ farmers work to actively change the way they farm by increasing biodiversity, enriching soils and enhancing the health of livestock and wildlife.

programme.title: RAZOR: DNA Archaeology
programme.desc: In this edition of RAZOR, Neil Cairns investigates a breakthrough known as "DNA archaeology" — a non-destructive way of retrieving ancient human DNA from Paleolithic tools. At La Grande Roche cave in France, archaeologist Dr. Marie Soressi shows how stone artifacts can now be directly linked to the people who once used them. In Leipzig, geneticist Elena Essel demonstrates the delicate extraction process inside a contamination-free clean lab, soaking artifacts in a phosphate buffer to release microscopic DNA fragments without damaging the object. Finally, Dr. Matthias Meyer analyses the sequences against modern reference genomes, uncovering details of sex, ancestry, and social patterns in Ice Age communities. For the first time, scientists are connecting tools not just to cultures, but to individuals — transforming our understanding of ancient human life.

programme.title: No Power, No AI
programme.desc: Is energy the ultimate bottleneck for artificial intelligence? As compute demand skyrockets, can our power grids keep up? CGTN's Michael Wang moderates a critical panel at the 2026 Summer Davos Forum on the intersection of AI and energy infrastructure. We'll explore how to align capital investment, infrastructure build-out, and policy frameworks; future-proof energy supply systems to strengthen energy security; and build a more inclusive, sustainable AI-powered energy future. Tune in as global leaders tackle the energy challenge powering the next wave of AI. #SummerDavos2026

programme.title: What's under the hood of a quantum computer?
programme.desc: Leading scientists have been seeking a new holy grail – "quantum supremacy." The term "quantum supremacy" does not indicate any technological superiority of one country over another, but rather the enormous computing advantage of quantum computers over classical computers, the latter can vary from the office laptop you type on to those supercomputers that require an entire building to house.

programme.title: New life for world cultural heritage
programme.desc: A sad day for humanity as a massive fire spread throughout Paris' Notre-Dame Cathedral on Monday. The blaze gulf the building's spire and roof to collapse. The Paris landmark has seen numerous restorations in its 800-plus-year history, but this time, a rebuild is needed, as pledged by France President Emmanuel Macron. But how? The only news not that bad today is that for years, architectural historians worked with laser scanners to capture the entirety of the cathedral's interior and exterior in meticulous 3D point clouds, a technology called 3D mapping. In season 2 of CGTN's original series Tech It Out, we reported this technology used by scientists to protect fragile world heritage in China. We hope this will make your day a little bit easier.

From a green beef cycle near Beijing to rice-prawn co-culture in Cambodia, this episode explores how technology, industry and cooperation are helping land thrive and livelihoods grow.

programme.title: Football expert: AI will never replace referees or players
programme.desc: Artificial intelligence (AI) is playing an increasingly important role at the 2026 World Cup. However, Chris Van Puyvelde, Chinese Football Association Technical Director, said that AI as a tool will never replace players or referees, as the human brain is the ultimate decision-maker in football, in a #Bizpodcast show with CGTN. #BizFocus

programme.title: Biztalk Special Coverage on AI EP3
programme.desc: The Token Economy Demand for AI tokens is surging, with trends like “tokenmaxxing” accelerating the race for computing power. As pressure mounts on computing and energy infrastructure, China is leveraging its clean energy strengths to support AI growth, while “token exports” emerge as a new way to deliver AI services to global users. What’s the future like for the “token economy”? Join us on BizTalk as we explore the economics behind the AI revolution.

programme.title: Global Business-Asian Edition Ep.1
programme.desc: Global Business is the flagship business show of CGTN English Channel, bringing you the latest economic data, business trends and policies.

programme.title: Fun Guide to China: Hainan 1
programme.desc: As Hainan moves towards implementing its special customs operation, CGTN’s Travelogue is launching a brand-new series, “Fun Guide to China”. Set in Hainan, the pilot episode journeys through its cities, mountains, coastlines and rainforests, capturing the vibrant atmosphere of life in the Free Trade Port.From the world’s largest stand-alone duty-free mall – a true shopper’s paradise, to the laid-back charm of an “Old Dad Tea” restaurant, host Tianran He explores the evolving cityscape of Haikou. On the northeast coast in Wenchang, he joins a local photographer to witness the launch of a Chinese space rocket, before diving into interactive space museums that bring the cosmos within reach. The journey then heads west to Danzhou, where campervans and “mermaids” share the same stage, before reaching Wanning on the east coast, where Tianran catches waves at one of China’s most famous surfing hotspots.

programme.title: Harmony Keepers: Guardians of black-necked cranes
programme.desc: This documentary series pays tribute to the guardians of China’s rare wildlife and their habitats. Through the lens of ecological protection and preservation, the series features four iconic protected species in China: the Tibetan antelope, milu deer, black-necked cranes, and golden snub-nosed monkeys.

programme.title: Foodwise: Great Walls of Beijing
programme.desc: The Great Wall of China is a popular tourist destination, but did you know that in Beijing, it's where locals go to unwind? From feasts of tofu made using well-water and fish caught fresh from the reservoir, to bubbling cauldrons of thinly-sliced lamb, the hilly suburbs of Beijing are home to some of the city's best-loved weekend escapes - and they're all by the foot of the Great Wall.

programme.title: Foodwise: How to Order Chinese Food
programme.desc: How do you order Chinese food? What's the best way to pair dishes? And what's up with the naming system? In this episode, hosts Tianran and Eva will give you a crash course on everything from deciphering the menu and choosing the right restaurant, to pairing dishes according to color, aroma, taste, and form, so that by the end of the show, you too can feast your friends like a pro.

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.6
programme.desc: The mountainous realm of Fujian's centers around the Wuyi range, just opposite Taiwan Province. Join us as we trace a heritage of tea production in ancient villages, marvel at waterside Danxia, and discover terraces where seas of lotuses grow...

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.6
programme.desc: The mountainous realm of Fujian's centers around the Wuyi range, just opposite Taiwan Province. Join us as we trace a heritage of tea production in ancient villages, marvel at waterside Danxia, and discover terraces where seas of lotuses grow...

programme.title: Harmony Keepers: Guardians of black-necked cranes
programme.desc: This documentary series pays tribute to the guardians of China’s rare wildlife and their habitats. Through the lens of ecological protection and preservation, the series features four iconic protected species in China: the Tibetan antelope, milu deer, black-necked cranes, and golden snub-nosed monkeys.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Pak Mei
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber seeks out Liu Weixin, a master of Pak Mei kungfu and on whom the hit game "Sifu" was based. He experiences for himself this style's relentless and lightning-fast strikes, but also learns from its master how to balance life and kungfu, how a master should treat his disciples, and what it means to "eat porridge at night"

programme.title: Wenzhou
programme.desc: Tianran He is in Wenzhou, southeast China — a city famed for its self-made millionaires. Here he meets the first Chinese shoemaker to expand overseas, and hears the stories of other local entrepreneurs. Along the way, he samples traditional snacks, visits some of China’s oldest lighthouses, and enjoys an evening of Ou Opera. He also learns the craft of papermaking, before heading into the hills to soak in hot springs and wander across ancient covered bridges.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-1 Baji Quan
programme.desc: In the first episode of Season 2 of Kung Fu Wise, Baji Quan, host Kleber travels to Mengcun, Cangzhou, in Hebei Province. There, he meets Wu Hao, the young head of the Wu-style Baji Quan school. Together, they journey across the North China Plain, from bustling village markets and the family training hall to the Bohai Sea coast, as Kleber experiences Baji Quan’s explosive power first-hand. Along the way, the episode unpacks the idea of “opening the gate”, the key to focused power, and the spirit of spear work. It goes beyond technique to reveal Cangzhou’s rich martial heritage, tracing how Wu-style Baji Quan has travelled from its hometown to Europe, proof that traditional kung fu can endure, evolve, and thrive today. In the end, Wu Hao answers with action: true inheritance is not only about skills, but about shaping spirit and character.

programme.title: Miao Silverware: A Shining Epic
programme.desc: In this episode, we explore the fascinating world of the silver-working art of the Miao ethnic group. Join us, and hear the stories and see the works of four remarkable individuals. From a national-level inheritor who is preserving the traditional craft, to an artist infusing personal flair into his designs; from a female silversmith imbuing her work with feminine warmth, to a young artisan inspiring a new generation, we investigate the rich heritage, cultural significance, and evolving potential of Miao silverware.

programme.title: China Untapped: Xizang Ep.4
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He travels to Shannan, the cradle of Tibetan culture. Nestled in the gentlest valley of the Yarlung Zangbo River, this is where the first Tibetan king rose to power, the first palace in Xizang was built, and many other “firsts” in Xizang’s history began. Tianran visits Yamdrok Lake, one of Xizang’s three sacred lakes, where a paragliding base is taking shape. He then learns the art of Tibetan opera, joins rangers on a mission to track the elusive Tibetan red deer, and ends the day with a rejuvenating soak in a village revitalized by hot spring tourism.

programme.title: Fun Guide to China: Hainan 1

programme.desc: The mountainous realm of Fujian's centers around the Wuyi range, just opposite Taiwan Province. Join us as we trace a heritage of tea production in ancient villages, marvel at waterside Danxia, and discover terraces where seas of lotuses grow...

programme.title: Harmony Keepers: Guardians of black-necked cranes
programme.desc: This documentary series pays tribute to the guardians of China’s rare wildlife and their habitats. Through the lens of ecological protection and preservation, the series features four iconic protected species in China: the Tibetan antelope, milu deer, black-necked cranes, and golden snub-nosed monkeys.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Pak Mei
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber seeks out Liu Weixin, a master of Pak Mei kungfu and on whom the hit game "Sifu" was based. He experiences for himself this style's relentless and lightning-fast strikes, but also learns from its master how to balance life and kungfu, how a master should treat his disciples, and what it means to "eat porridge at night"

programme.title: Wenzhou
programme.desc: Tianran He is in Wenzhou, southeast China — a city famed for its self-made millionaires. Here he meets the first Chinese shoemaker to expand overseas, and hears the stories of other local entrepreneurs. Along the way, he samples traditional snacks, visits some of China’s oldest lighthouses, and enjoys an evening of Ou Opera. He also learns the craft of papermaking, before heading into the hills to soak in hot springs and wander across ancient covered bridges.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-2 Chinese Wrestling
programme.desc: In Episode 2, Chinese Wrestling, host Kleber joins a public training session led by Wang Tongqing, a master preserving the imperial Shanpu Ying wrestling tradition. Swept up in the group’s lively atmosphere, he gets a first-hand feel for the master’s skill. Kleber then invites his Italian friend Paolo to train systematically, uncovering how Chinese wrestling principles connect with other martial arts, from Tai Chi to Wing Chun. Through Master Wang’s story of bringing a formerly imperial martial art into the public sphere, mentoring the next generation, and hoping to take it onto the international stage, the episode shows wrestling as more than a test of strength. It is a cultural bridge between history and the present.

programme.title: Exploring Ethnic Attire: Dong Elegance
programme.desc: Dong clothing weaves together tradition, craftsmanship, and the unique identity of the Dong ethnic group. In this episode, we’ll unravel the stories behind the intricate designs, vibrant colors, and deep cultural significance of Dong attire. From ceremonial garments to everyday wear, each piece is imbued with the essence of a rich heritage.

programme.title: China Untapped: Xizang Ep.5
programme.desc: In this final episode, Tianran journeys to Nyingchi, known as “Xizang’s Garden.” Here, he experiences the region’s semi-pastoral way of life — feeding yaks, trying his hand at archery, and seeing how tourism is transforming lives in Lulang International Tourist Town. Travelling along National Highway 318, Tianran discovers towering millennia-old cypress trees and rafts through the vast Yani National Wetland Park, before ending the day with a unique spa treatment at a traditional Tibetan hospital.

programme.title: Fun Guide to China: Hainan 2
programme.desc: In this episode host Tianran He continues his adventure across Hainan. This time, the road trip begins in Dongfang, where Tianran tucks into roast suckling pig by day and, by night, attends grassroots basketball games that rival NBA games in atmosphere. In the mountains of Wuzhishan, Tianran explores a lush jungle with forest rangers, before stepping back in time to 1988 for a retro-chic disco night. In Lingshui, the journey moves offshore as Tianran enjoys a seafood feast on a floating fishing platform, then heads back to shore to visit a contemporary art museum by the pier. After reaching Sanya in the south, he joins scuba divers on a mission to restore coral reefs, before winding down on a yacht with friends and laughter. Finally, Tianran travels to Baisha for the “La’aomen” harvest festival, where the local ethnic Li people sing, dance and celebrate their most important crop: “Mountain Orchid Rice”.

programme.title: Harmony Keepers: Guardians of milu deer
programme.desc: This documentary series pays tribute to the guardians of China’s rare wildlife and their habitats. Through the lens of ecological protection and preservation, the series features four iconic protected species in China: the Tibetan antelope, milu deer, black-necked cranes, and golden snub-nosed monkeys.

programme.title: Foodwise: Eat Like Confucius
programme.desc: "Foodwise" host Tianran He explores the ancient town of Qufu, the birthplace of Confucius. Together with a 75th generation descendant of the sage himself, they explore one of Confucius' lifelong pursuits: To cultivate oneself for the betterment of others. From the sophisticated cooking techniques and dining etiquette of Confucian cuisine, which was used for banqueting emperors and aristocrats, to getting hands on with street food and local delicacies, Tianran explores how Confucius' ideals have permeated through modern day life in Qufu.

programme.title: Foodwise: The Herbal Way of Life
programme.desc: 'Medicine tonic is not as good as food tonic' - this Chinese saying is a core principle of Traditional Chinese Medicine. It is believed that the right diet and nourishment can cure and reverse the effects of disease, hence considered an inevitable source of natural health preservation. In this Foodwise episode, our hosts Tianran He and Eva May explore this ancient concept of medicine food homology as they taste their way through different herbs, chat to experts of Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) and visit venues that combine conventional principles with modern ideas. Dive deep into the origin of TCM with us as we learn about the harmony between man and nature and its application in modern functional foods!

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.7
programme.desc: Jiangsu---an aquatic lowland stitched together by the Yangtze and Grand Canal. From Yellow Sea wetlands populated by red-crowned cranes to the Yangtze estuary's port history, this is a land and coast where maritime legends are made!

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.7

Next, Kleber meets a master of Choy Li Fut, a kungfu style that utilizes everyday items such as towels, fans and even abacuses, and discovers the link between martial arts and lion dance.

programme.desc: Jiangsu---an aquatic lowland stitched together by the Yangtze and Grand Canal. From Yellow Sea wetlands populated by red-crowned cranes to the Yangtze estuary's port history, this is a land and coast where maritime legends are made!

programme.title: Harmony Keepers: Guardians of milu deer
programme.desc: This documentary series pays tribute to the guardians of China’s rare wildlife and their habitats. Through the lens of ecological protection and preservation, the series features four iconic protected species in China: the Tibetan antelope, milu deer, black-necked cranes, and golden snub-nosed monkeys.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Hung Ga and Choy Li Fut
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber meets with two masters of southern Chinese martial arts. He learns from a Hung Ga master that while this kungfu style is often associated with brute force, it’s actually a combination of hard and soft techniques that can also be used in traditional Chinese massage. Next, Kleber meets a master of Choy Li Fut, a kungfu style that utilizes everyday items such as towels, fans and even abacuses, and discovers the link between martial arts and lion dance.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-3 Shaolin Style
programme.desc: Episode 3, Shaolin, explores how this thousand-year-old temple has evolved into a unique “kung fu platform” that draws enthusiasts from around the world. Host Kleber immerses himself in Shaolin’s integrated way of life, where Chan (Zen) practice and martial training go hand in hand, and turns the camera toward foreign practitioners pursuing their kung fu dreams both inside and beyond the temple gates. Along the way, he meets international disciples living and training at Shaolin, as well as students learning the rare art of Shaolin Xinyiba under Master Hu Zhengsheng, a former Shaolin monk. At martial arts schools near Songshan Mountain, Kleber also encounters overseas competitors training hard for the arena. Together, their stories reveal Shaolin kung fu’s enduring pull, one that crosses cultures and borders while uniting practitioners through tradition, discipline, and self-mastery.

programme.title: Crossroads of Cultures: The Legacy of Silk Roads
programme.desc: Quanzhou, situated on the southeastern coast of China, once served as a maritime hub of the East and Southeast Asia trade network during the 10th to 14th centuries, gaining prominence for its exceptional trading port. With 22 distinctive heritage sites listed on the UNESCO World Heritage List, this city stands as a testament to the legendary history of the Silk Roads. In this program, we invite four individuals living in Quanzhou to showcase their daily lives, capturing their journeys through the city's world heritage sites. Through their perspectives, we present a dynamic portrayal of a city rich in heritage. From the maritime splendor of Quanzhou's history to the serene echoes of its ancient city, from the historical treasures of the Luoyang Bridge to the vibrancy of the modern urban landscape, we journey alongside our guests through time and space, exploring Quanzhou's diverse charm and unique allure. Join us as we immerse ourselves in a city that bridges history and cultures, and embraces innovation.

programme.title: China Bound: Guangzhou
programme.desc: Join us in Guangzhou, a global manufacturing hub in south China with a population as large as New York’s. From browsing hip street markets to wholesale clothes shopping, sampling world-famous Cantonese cuisine, and visiting the childhood home of kungfu legend Bruce Lee, there is plenty to do during our stay.

programme.title: China Untapped: Xizang Ep.3
programme.desc: Join Tianran He on a high-speed train to Shigatse, gateway to the Himalayas and home to Mount Qomolangma, the world’s highest peak. As the city with the most land ports in Xizang, Shigatse serves as a key corridor to South Asia. Here, Tianran samples breakfast at a bustling local teahouse and experiences monastic life at the vast Tashilunpo Monastery. He then visits an agricultural park where tropical crops thrive against the odds of the alpine plateau, before discovering pulu — a traditional Tibetan textile now earning global acclaim.

programme.title: Young drummer of Ansai
programme.desc: Soaring leaps and the energetic beats of the Ansai waist drum dance made Gao Yuchen, a nine-year-old boy, fall in love with the dance at first sight. Growing up in Ansai, Shaanxi, Gao was first drawn to the large-scale folk dance while watching a local performance. As a form of intangible cultural heritage, the Ansai waist drum dance was promoted by local authorities and was included as part of his school's PE program. Gao was able to receive waist drum dance training as an extracurricular activity at his school, and he subsequently became a rising star on campus. He says he wants to become a better dancer and hopes to perform on bigger stages.

programme.title: Foodwise: Beijing
programme.desc: From the local delicacies of Beijing's Ox Street to latest hangouts and contemporary cuisine of the Chinese capital, hosts Tianran He and Eva May will give you a true taste of Beijing, and the fascinating stories and people behind them.

programme.title: Foodwise: Beijing's Hutongs
programme.desc: The Hutongs in Beijing are considered the city's blood vessels, as these traditional alleyways make up the DNA of the capital for its rich history and culture. In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May zigzag their way through the Hutongs and the Forbidden City for a mouth-watering experience full of ancient and modern delicacies. Starring German designer Katrin, Russian artist Liuba and photographer Feng Yu from Beijing, our hosts are taking you along a journey that features fried noodles, dim sum, pancakes and co.!

programme.title: A Village Homestay in Beijing
programme.desc: In this episode of Travelogue, our presenter Tianran He’s heading out of Beijing proper, to the city’s Miyun District. There he’ll meet some people running guesthouses who gave up their day jobs, in order to achieve the dream of a place of their own in the country.

programme.title: Diabolo
programme.desc: In China, the diabolo has a history of over 600 years. From the earliest uniaxial design, it developed to have a double axis. With time, it came to be made in various shapes and from various materials. And its popularity began to spread around the world. In the hands of an expert, a diabolo can create a truly mesmerizing spectacle. So, let’s hear from a professional about the various tricks of his trade.

programme.title: The Spirit of Zhaxidele
programme.desc: In this episode of Travelogue, Tianran backpacks the way to the western reaches of China. Making his home in beautiful Jiaju village, he learns just what it takes to be a Tibetan, then reaches the mysterious village of Suopo and explore its ancient watchtowers.

programme.title: Young drummer of Ansai
programme.desc: Soaring leaps and the energetic beats of the Ansai waist drum dance made Gao Yuchen, a nine-year-old boy, fall in love with the dance at first sight. Growing up in Ansai, Shaanxi, Gao was first drawn to the large-scale folk dance while watching a local performance. As a form of intangible cultural heritage, the Ansai waist drum dance was promoted by local authorities and was included as part of his school's PE program. Gao was able to receive waist drum dance training as an extracurricular activity at his school, and he subsequently became a rising star on campus. He says he wants to become a better dancer and hopes to perform on bigger stages.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Taiji Quan
programme.desc: Kleber travels to Chenjiagou in central China, the birthplace of Chen-style Taiji Quan. Also known as tai chi, the martial art is iconic for its slow movements and purported health benefits, but Kleber quickly discovers it’s the real deal: From being thrown around by teenagers to learning about the martial art’s Yin-Yang philosophy, Kleber experiences for himself how this kungfu style allows small to defeat big, and soft to beat strong.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-3 Shaolin Style
programme.desc: Episode 3, Shaolin, explores how this thousand-year-old temple has evolved into a unique “kung fu platform” that draws enthusiasts from around the world. Host Kleber immerses himself in Shaolin’s integrated way of life, where Chan (Zen) practice and martial training go hand in hand, and turns the camera toward foreign practitioners pursuing their kung fu dreams both inside and beyond the temple gates. Along the way, he meets international disciples living and training at Shaolin, as well as students learning the rare art of Shaolin Xinyiba under Master Hu Zhengsheng, a former Shaolin monk. At martial arts schools near Songshan Mountain, Kleber also encounters overseas competitors training hard for the arena. Together, their stories reveal Shaolin kung fu’s enduring pull, one that crosses cultures and borders while uniting practitioners through tradition, discipline, and self-mastery.

programme.title: Crossroads of Cultures: The Legacy of Silk Roads
programme.desc: Quanzhou, situated on the southeastern coast of China, once served as a maritime hub of the East and Southeast Asia trade network during the 10th to 14th centuries, gaining prominence for its exceptional trading port. With 22 distinctive heritage sites listed on the UNESCO World Heritage List, this city stands as a testament to the legendary history of the Silk Roads. In this program, we invite four individuals living in Quanzhou to showcase their daily lives, capturing their journeys through the city's world heritage sites. Through their perspectives, we present a dynamic portrayal of a city rich in heritage. From the maritime splendor of Quanzhou's history to the serene echoes of its ancient city, from the historical treasures of the Luoyang Bridge to the vibrancy of the modern urban landscape, we journey alongside our guests through time and space, exploring Quanzhou's diverse charm and unique allure. Join us as we immerse ourselves in a city that bridges history and cultures, and embraces innovation.

programme.title: China Bound: Guangzhou
programme.desc: Join us in Guangzhou, a global manufacturing hub in south China with a population as large as New York’s. From browsing hip street markets to wholesale clothes shopping, sampling world-famous Cantonese cuisine, and visiting the childhood home of kungfu legend Bruce Lee, there is plenty to do during our stay.

programme.title: China Untapped: Xizang Ep.3
programme.desc: Join Tianran He on a high-speed train to Shigatse, gateway to the Himalayas and home to Mount Qomolangma, the world’s highest peak. As the city with the most land ports in Xizang, Shigatse serves as a key corridor to South Asia. Here, Tianran samples breakfast at a bustling local teahouse and experiences monastic life at the vast Tashilunpo Monastery. He then visits an agricultural park where tropical crops thrive against the odds of the alpine plateau, before discovering pulu — a traditional Tibetan textile now earning global acclaim.

programme.title: Young drummer of Ansai
programme.desc: Soaring leaps and the energetic beats of the Ansai waist drum dance made Gao Yuchen, a nine-year-old boy, fall in love with the dance at first sight. Growing up in Ansai, Shaanxi, Gao was first drawn to the large-scale folk dance while watching a local performance. As a form of intangible cultural heritage, the Ansai waist drum dance was promoted by local authorities and was included as part of his school's PE program. Gao was able to receive waist drum dance training as an extracurricular activity at his school, and he subsequently became a rising star on campus. He says he wants to become a better dancer and hopes to perform on bigger stages.

programme.title: Foodwise: Beijing
programme.desc: From the local delicacies of Beijing's Ox Street to latest hangouts and contemporary cuisine of the Chinese capital, hosts Tianran He and Eva May will give you a true taste of Beijing, and the fascinating stories and people behind them.

programme.title: Foodwise: Beijing's Hutongs
programme.desc: The Hutongs in Beijing are considered the city's blood vessels, as these traditional alleyways make up the DNA of the capital for its rich history and culture. In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May zigzag their way through the Hutongs and the Forbidden City for a mouth-watering experience full of ancient and modern delicacies. Starring German designer Katrin, Russian artist Liuba and photographer Feng Yu from Beijing, our hosts are taking you along a journey that features fried noodles, dim sum, pancakes and co.!

programme.title: A Village Homestay in Beijing
programme.desc: In this episode of Travelogue, our presenter Tianran He’s heading out of Beijing proper, to the city’s Miyun District. There he’ll meet some people running guesthouses who gave up their day jobs, in order to achieve the dream of a place of their own in the country.

programme.title: Diabolo
programme.desc: In China, the diabolo has a history of over 600 years. From the earliest uniaxial design, it developed to have a double axis. With time, it came to be made in various shapes and from various materials. And its popularity began to spread around the world. In the hands of an expert, a diabolo can create a truly mesmerizing spectacle. So, let’s hear from a professional about the various tricks of his trade.

programme.title: The Spirit of Zhaxidele
programme.desc: In this episode of Travelogue, Tianran backpacks the way to the western reaches of China. Making his home in beautiful Jiaju village, he learns just what it takes to be a Tibetan, then reaches the mysterious village of Suopo and explore its ancient watchtowers.

programme.title: Young drummer of Ansai
programme.desc: Soaring leaps and the energetic beats of the Ansai waist drum dance made Gao Yuchen, a nine-year-old boy, fall in love with the dance at first sight. Growing up in Ansai, Shaanxi, Gao was first drawn to the large-scale folk dance while watching a local performance. As a form of intangible cultural heritage, the Ansai waist drum dance was promoted by local authorities and was included as part of his school's PE program. Gao was able to receive waist drum dance training as an extracurricular activity at his school, and he subsequently became a rising star on campus. He says he wants to become a better dancer and hopes to perform on bigger stages.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Taiji Quan
programme.desc: Kleber travels to Chenjiagou in central China, the birthplace of Chen-style Taiji Quan. Also known as tai chi, the martial art is iconic for its slow movements and purported health benefits, but Kleber quickly discovers it’s the real deal: From being thrown around by teenagers to learning about the martial art’s Yin-Yang philosophy, Kleber experiences for himself how this kungfu style allows small to defeat big, and soft to beat strong.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-3 Shaolin Style
programme.desc: Episode 3, Shaolin, explores how this thousand-year-old temple has evolved into a unique “kung fu platform” that draws enthusiasts from around the world. Host Kleber immerses himself in Shaolin’s integrated way of life, where Chan (Zen) practice and martial training go hand in hand, and turns the camera toward foreign practitioners pursuing their kung fu dreams both inside and beyond the temple gates. Along the way, he meets international disciples living and training at Shaolin, as well as students learning the rare art of Shaolin Xinyiba under Master Hu Zhengsheng, a former Shaolin monk. At martial arts schools near Songshan Mountain, Kleber also encounters overseas competitors training hard for the arena. Together, their stories reveal Shaolin kung fu’s enduring pull, one that crosses cultures and borders while uniting practitioners through tradition, discipline, and self-mastery.
